methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
DNA Script is a Series C–stage biotech company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Ultima Genomics, Insitro, Colossal Biosciences) and 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to $200M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies $340M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage private comparables yields $2.4B, with a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as DNA Script's revenue is not disclosed. Triangulating these two methods produces a low of $289M, median of $1.4B, and high of $2.8B.

The median implied valuation of $1.4B reflects the tension between stage-progression mechanics and peer benchmarking. The wide spread ($289M–$2.8B) reflects meaningful uncertainty around DNA Script's technology maturity, revenue trajectory, and competitive positioning relative to peers with disparate valuations. Further diligence on revenue scale and gross margins would narrow this range considerably.
